name: RandomTree

unions.name: RandomTree
unions.comment: Type that represents binary tree with weighted elements for random picking up.

aliases.name: Weighted
aliases.comment: Type alias that represents each weighted contents in `RandomTree`.

aliases.name: WideFloat
aliases.comment:
Type that represent floating point number with wider range than `Float` in core package. This is introduced for preventing overflow of exponentially changing value.
If you need to directly manipulate values of `WideFloat`, please hit `elm install kudzu-forest/elm-wide-float` in your terminal.

values.name: adjustWeight
values.comment: Returns weighted datum whose weight is multiplied with the given `Float`.

values.name: count
values.comment: Returns how many elements the tree has.

values.name: delete
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which is the same as the first parameter from the second parameter. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe`.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which passes the test function given as the first parameter from the second parameter. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe`.
values.type: (RandomTree.WideFloat -> a -> Basics.Bool) -> RandomTree.RandomTree a -> Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.RandomTree a)

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Returns a `RandomTree` that has all element in given list.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted a -> List.List (RandomTree.Weighted a) -> RandomTree.RandomTree a

values.name: fromPairs
values.comment:
Creates `RandomTree` from list of tuples of a `Float` value corresponding to its weight as the first component, and a datum as the second component.
the weighter it is, the higher probability it is chosen in.
values.type: ( Basics.Float, a ) -> List.List ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.RandomTree a

values.name: get
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `RandomTree` given as parameter.
The time complexity is _O(log(N))_ where _N_ denotes the size of the tree.

values.name: insert
values.comment: Inserts a Weighted data to an already-existing `RandomTree`.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted a -> RandomTree.RandomTree a -> RandomTree.RandomTree a

values.name: insertListWithRelativeWeight
values.comment: Inserts all elements of given list into the tree of second parameter. The first components of the tuple denotes the relative weight(`1` means the whole weight of the original tree).
values.type: List.List ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.RandomTree a -> RandomTree.RandomTree a

values.name: insertWithRelativeWeight
values.comment: Inserts an element into the tree. The first parameter denotes the relative weight(`1` means the whole weight of the original tree).
values.type: Basics.Float -> a -> RandomTree.RandomTree a -> RandomTree.RandomTree a

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps all the content of the tree, with the weights unchanged.

values.name: replace
values.comment: Random generator that replaces one weighted data from the tree and generates a pair consisting of the removed data and resultant tree.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted a -> RandomTree.RandomTree a -> Random.Generator ( RandomTree.Weighted a, RandomTree.RandomTree a )

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Returns a `RandomTree` that has only one element.

values.name: take
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `RandomTree`, paired with the rest part of the tree.
If the `RandomTree` has only one element, then `Nothing` is returned as the second component.
The time complexity is _O(log(N))_ where _N_ denotes the size of the tree.
values.type: RandomTree.RandomTree a -> Random.Generator ( RandomTree.Weighted a, Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.RandomTree a) )

values.name: totalWeight
values.comment: Returns the sum of weight of all the elements in the tree.
